summary: Completed end-to-end testing (backend + frontend) of Subscription Overload Manager. All 21 pytest backend tests pass (100%). Frontend flows work except for one integration bug: after adding a subscription via the header/sidebar Add button (which opens the AppShell-global modal), Subscriptions list and Dashboard do not auto-refresh — data is persisted but UI shows stale state until a reload/navigation.

integration_issues.flow: Add subscription via global modal (header 'Add subscription' / sidebar 'New subscription' buttons on Subscriptions page)
integration_issues.issue: After POST /subscriptions succeeds and toast 'Subscription added.' shows, the Subscriptions list remains empty and Dashboard counts stay at 0 until a full page reload or navigation away-and-back. Root cause: AppShell.jsx line 153 renders SubscriptionModal with onSaved={() => setAddOpen(false)} — it only closes the modal; it does not notify Subscriptions.jsx or Dashboard.jsx to refetch. (In contrast, the EDIT flow at Subscriptions.jsx line 190 correctly calls load() in onSaved.)

action_items: Fix stale state after adding a subscription from the global (AppShell) modal. Simplest fix: pass a refresh callback via useOutletContext (e.g., register a setter from Subscriptions/Dashboard) or use a lightweight event bus / shared React context so onSaved triggers a refetch on Subscriptions list AND Dashboard summary. Alternative: have Subscriptions.jsx render its own add-modal instead of relying on AppShell's global modal, mirroring how the edit flow already works (line 190).
critical_code_review_comments: AppShell.jsx (line 153): the global SubscriptionModal's onSaved={() => setAddOpen(false)} does not propagate a 'data changed' signal to children; this causes stale UI on Subscriptions + Dashboard after add.
Edit vs Add modals are duplicated: Subscriptions.jsx renders a second SubscriptionModal (line 190) only for edit — consider consolidating into a single shared data-refresh mechanism.

Backend /api/recommendations can emit duplicate 'reminder' suggestions for subs renewing ≤3 days if they also match the upcoming-renewal block — not incorrect, but consider deduping by subscription_id + type for cleaner UI.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all flows work; 1 UX integration bug on add-subscription refresh)

test_credentials: demo@example.com / demo123 (confirmed working). Fresh registrations also working.
seed_data_creation: Tests register TEST_<uuid>@example.com users with Netflix/AWS/Figma subscriptions. Leftover DB records are minimal and scoped to their own user_ids.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 21 backend pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py pass. Only remaining issue is the frontend refresh bug on add-subscription (see integration_issues). After main agent fixes AppShell's SubscriptionModal onSaved callback to propagate a reload, re-run the Playwright script: register new user → Subscriptions page → click 'New subscription' → fill Netflix/649/future date → Save → assert Netflix appears in the list WITHOUT reload. Also verify Dashboard KPIs update without reload.
rca_of_the_issue: Bug: Adding a subscription from header/sidebar global modal leaves UI stale. Reproduction: (1) register fresh user, (2) go to /app/subscriptions, (3) click New subscription, (4) fill form and save → toast shows 'Subscription added.' but 'Showing 0' persists. (5) reload page → sub now appears. RCA: AppShell.jsx line 153 wires onSaved to only close the modal. Subscriptions.jsx and Dashboard.jsx each fetch data in their own useEffect and have no subscription to a refresh event. Mitigation: expose a refreshKey/bumpRefresh function via useOutletContext from AppShell and re-run load() when it changes; or emit a window event ('som:subs-changed') and have Subscriptions/Dashboard listen via useEffect.
